title: uinstnew
text: uinstnew Create a new instance of a Uniface 4GL component. Explain this code long uinstnew (UHACThEnv, UCHAR*compName, UCHAR*compID, UCHAR*instName, intmode, UCHAR*propList, UHACT*phInst, long*pProcError); Copy Parameters hEnv Environment handle as returned by uecreate or ueopen . compName Name of the component to be instantiated. compID Component ID of the component specified in compName . This parameter is optional. If you provide a component ID, it will be checked against the ID of the compiled signature in the UAR file. instName Name of the instance. This parameter is optional. mode Communication mode of the instance. Allowed values are: UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE —mode as specified in the signature definition USYNC_COMM_MODE —synchronous mode UASYNC_COMM_MODE —asynchronous mode propList An optional, zero-separated list of instance properties. This mimics the functionality of the ProcScript command newinstance .Multiple instance properties must be separated by a zero character, and the list must he terminated by two zero characters, for example transaction=F/0attached/0/0 . phInst (Output) the instance handle. pProcError (Output) the ProcScript error status, that is, the same value that would be returned by newinstance in $procerror . If you are not interested in this output, supply a zero value instead of a pointer. Return Values See the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic. Use Library: ucall Description uinstnew is the 3GL counterpart of the ProcScript command newinstance . The functionality is largely the same, except that uinstnew works only with a handle, whereas newinstance can also work with an instance name. Your application is responsible for deleting the instance, as well as freeing the instance handle. Typically, you would use the following call sequence: Explain this code uinstnew(hEnv, "SVC", 0, 0, /* Create the instance */ UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hInst, 0); /* Do something with the instance ... */ uinstdel(hInst, 0); /* Destroy the instance */ ufreeh(&hInst); /* Free the instance handle */ Copy Example: uecreate, uedelete, ufreeh, uinstdel, uinstnew, uinstopr, and uopract The following simple call-in program shows how to activate the EXEC operation of the service MYSVC. As usual, error checking has been omitted for the sake of brevity. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> void main (void) { UHACT hEnv; /* Uniface environment */ UHACT hIn; /* Instance handle */ UHACT hOp; /* Operation handle */ long opStat; /* Activate status return parameter */ /* Create the environment */ uecreate(1, 0, 0, "my.asn", 0, 0, &hEnv); /* Instantiate service MYSVC */ uinstnew(hEnv, "MYSVC", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, 0); uinstopr(hIn, "EXEC", 0, &hOp); /* Instantiate the EXEC operation */ uopract (hOp, &opStat, 0); /* Activate the operation */ uinstdel(hIn, 0); /* Destroy the instance */ ufreeh (&hOp); /* Free the operation */ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free the instance */ uedelete(hEnv, -1); /* Shut down the environment */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free the environment handle */ return 0; // on success } Copy Related Links Call-In API Return Codes Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise. uecreate Initialize the Uniface runtime for component call-in. ufreeh Free (delete) a handle. uinstdel Delete a component instance. uinstopr Return the handle to an operation.
